de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.metaconstruct

Class WhileInvariantTransformation

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.java.visitor.JavaASTWalker
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.java.visitor.JavaASTVisitor
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.metaconstruct.WhileLoopTransformation
          • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.metaconstruct.WhileInvariantTransformation
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Visitor

    public class WhileInvariantTransformation
extends WhileLoopTransformation
    Walks through a java AST in depth-left-fist-order. This walker is used to transform a loop (not only while loops) according to the rules of the dynamic logic.

      • performActionOnEnhancedFor

        public void performActionOnEnhancedFor(EnhancedFor x)
        Transform the body of an enhanced for loop for usage with invariant-theorems. The following restriction is made for enhanced for loops: There is only one label (no inner/outer pair) needed, so innerlabel and outerlabel must be the same. The loop body is transformed like for a while loop (break/continue/return replaced, try+catch added). If the top loop is an enhancedFor-loop transform it like a while loop. Due to the fact that the condition in enhanced loops has no side effects, things can be put easier here. If the loop is not top most, act like the super class.
        Specified by:
        performActionOnEnhancedFor in interface Visitor
        Overrides:
        performActionOnEnhancedFor in class WhileLoopTransformation
